maritime.title: NERAI Maritime Weekly
events: Strait of Hormuz Near-Standstill as US-Iran War Drives Tanker Attacks
Houthis Resume Saudi Tanker Attacks, Forcing Vessels to Reverse Course in Red Sea
INTERTANKO Warns Shipping Threat Now Spans Entire Gulf Region
Oil Prices Surge Past $96; Goldman Warns Brent Could Top $120 on Dual-Chokepoint Crisis
Suspected Somali Pirates Seize Tanker Near Yemen Amid Wave of Hijackings
maritime.summary: The week was dominated by an intensifying US-Iran military confrontation that has pushed the Strait of Hormuz into a near-standstill, with vessel crossings falling to multi-week lows and multiple tankers struck or set ablaze. Simultaneously, the Houthis reopened a second front by threatening and attacking Saudi oil exports transiting the Red Sea and Bab el-Mandeb, forcing tankers to reverse course and reroute, compounding the dual-chokepoint energy risk. Together, these developments represent the most severe simultaneous disruption to Gulf and Red Sea shipping corridors in recent memory, with oil prices surging past $96 and Goldman Sachs warning Brent could exceed $120.

sanctions.title: NERAI Sanctions Weekly
events: Treasury Doubles Down on Iran: Shamkhani Shipping Empire and Weapons-Procurement Network Hit
EU's 21st Russia Sanctions Package Stalls as Member-State Divisions Deepen
U.S. Expands Cuba Sanctions, Alleges Spy Infiltration of Trump Administration
DOJ Indicts Three Russians for Hosting LockBit, Cl0p and Play Ransomware Infrastructure
OFAC VPN Designation Accidentally Disables Telegram Short Links Globally
sanctions.summary: This week's sanctions landscape was dominated by a sustained U.S. maximum-pressure campaign against Iran's illicit shipping and weapons-procurement networks, while Europe's internal divisions continued to stall the 21st Russia sanctions package and undermine enforcement coherence. Secondary threads — U.S. Cuba designations, UN action in eastern Congo, and the collateral disruption caused by a VPN-related OFAC designation — round out a week that illustrates both the expanding scope of unilateral U.S. sanctions and the persistent fragmentation of multilateral enforcement.
